samples.text: Most birds can fly because they have wings adapted for flight. A robin is a bird with wings adapted for flight. Can a robin fly?
The majority of birds are capable of flight due to their wing structure. A robin belongs to the group of birds with flight-capable wings. Is a robin able to fly?
The majority of avians are capable of airborne movement because they possess aerodynamic wings. A robin is an avian with aerodynamic wings. Is a robin capable of airborne movement?
A robin is classified as a typical bird. Typical birds possess wings adapted for flight and are capable of flying. Can a robin fly?
Most birds can fly because they have wings adapted for flight. A robin is a bird with wings adapted for flight. Robins are often seen in gardens. Can a robin fly?
